title: ensure_param_file: tmp file leaked if rename fails after successful write
description: wallet/src/params.rs ensure_param_file (line ~249): if std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &data) succeeds but std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, path) fails, the code correctly calls remove_file(&tmp_path) to clean up. However, if remove_file also fails (disk full, permissions, NFS), the tmp file (e.g. sapling-spend.params.tmp) is permanently leaked on disk. Trigger: full disk or permissions error during rename. Bad behavior: stale .params.tmp files accumulate and could be gigabytes (spend params are ~48MB). This is a minor but real maintenance issue; on retry the download will fail because write_file sees the old .params (deleted) but not the .tmp. The fix is to use a random suffix or process-ID suffix for the tmp file and clean it up on any error path including remove_file failure.
